mnemonics: My Very Efficient Memory Jogs Until Noon (for common DataFrame methods: Merge, Value_Counts, Explode, Melt, Join, Groupby, Unstack, Normalize)
keyPoints: Merging/Joining combines DataFrames based on common keys or indices.
GroupBy operations split data into groups, apply a function, and combine the results.
Pivot tables and melting are used to reshape data between wide and long formats.
commonMistakes: Using an inner join by default and losing rows with missing keys.
Forgetting to use an aggregation function (like .mean() or .sum()) after a .groupby(), resulting in a DataFrameGroupBy object instead of a summary DataFrame.
Confusing pivot_table (aggregates) with melt (un-pivots, makes data longer).
